critic_raw:
CRITIQUE — real_world_framing_009 (Multi-round, Run 2)

1. MERCHANT AGREEMENT IMPRESSION GUARANTEE BREACH: Contractual impression share minimums for merchants above the revenue threshold may be breached by the new model's concentration effect. Pre-deployment audit required.

2. CATALOG CONCENTRATION AND LONG-TAIL SUPPRESSION: Long-tail suppression creates compound catalog health effects not detectable in 2-week CTR measurement.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE — real_world_framing_009 (Multi-round, Run 2 — receives Critic output)

1. MERCHANT AGREEMENT: CONCEDE — compliance audit is mandatory before full deployment.
2. LONG-TAIL SUPPRESSION: PARTIAL CONCEDE — strategic concern with real compound effects. Constrained ranking is the appropriate technical fix.

debate_rounds.critic_move: PROPOSE EMPIRICAL TEST for long-tail suppression: Monitor impression share for bottom 50% of catalog items over 60-day post-deployment period. Critic wins if impression share for long-tail items continues declining (>10% additional reduction from A/B test baseline). Defense wins if impression share stabilizes within the A/B test observed range.
debate_rounds.defender_move: ACCEPT EMPIRICAL TEST. The monitoring approach is sound and directly tests whether the suppression effect compounds over time.

adjudication_raw: All issues resolved. Merchant agreement audit agreed in initial defense; long-tail monitoring empirical test agreed in round 1. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

empirical_test.condition: Merchant impression guarantee audit before deployment; 60-day post-deployment monitoring of long-tail impression share distribution.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: ≥5% of contracted merchants below thresholds, or long-tail impression share declines >10% from A/B test baseline.
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Merchant compliance met; long-tail impression share stabilizes.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Merchant compliance met but long-tail metrics show continued drift.
